description: Add interactive SVG circuit simulators to 6 key modules: DC (Ohm's law, series/parallel, voltage divider, power, EMF), Impedans (RLC circuit with animated current flow), Trefase (star/delta animated diagram), Voltage Drop (cable with animated current and voltage gradient), Short Circuit (fault current flow animation), and Motor Theory (rotor/stator animated diagram). Each simulator shows a circuit schematic with animated current flow (marching dashes), color-coded voltage potential (gradient from high to low), resistance/impedance visual sizing, and power dissipation indicators (heat glow). The simulators update instantly when the user clicks different values.
steps: Step 1 - Add CSS for simulator animations: Add new CSS classes after line ~640 in el-dimensionering.html for the simulator SVG elements. Classes needed: .sim-wire (animated marching dashes like sld-flow but with speed proportional to current), .sim-resistor (rectangle with heat glow proportional to power), .sim-source (voltage source circle with potential color), .sim-node (junction dot), .sim-label (value annotation text). Also add @keyframes simFlow for the dash animation. Respect prefers-reduced-motion and body.motion-off by adding overrides in those blocks.

Step 2 - DC Ohm's Law simulator (renderDCOhm): After the result-box in renderDCOhm() (~line 25975), add a new result-box containing an inline SVG circuit diagram. The diagram shows: a voltage source (circle with +/- and U value), a wire (animated with sld-flow-style marching dashes, speed proportional to current I), a resistor (rectangle colored from blue=cold to red=hot based on power P=I^2*R), and labels showing U, I, R, P values at their respective positions. The animation speed of the dashes should be inversely proportional to current (more current = faster dashes). Use a helper function renderDCOhmSim(U, R, I, P) to generate the SVG string.

Step 3 - DC Series/Parallel simulator (renderDCSeriesParallel): After the result-box, add an SVG showing resistors connected in series (stacked horizontally) or parallel (stacked vertically with connecting buses), with each resistor sized/colored proportionally to its value. Show current arrows and voltage labels at each node. Use renderDCSeriesParallelSim(mode, resistors, Rtotal) helper.

Step 4 - DC Voltage Divider simulator (renderDCDivider): Add SVG showing the classic voltage divider circuit - source on left, R1 on top, R2 on bottom, output tap in middle. Show voltage values at each node (Uin at top, Uout at middle, 0V at bottom). Animate current flow through both resistors. Color resistors by their value ratio. Use renderDCDividerSim(Uin, R1, R2, Uout, I) helper.

Step 5 - DC Power simulator (renderDCPower): Add SVG showing a simple load circuit with a power meter. The load element glows with intensity proportional to power dissipation. Show a power bar/gauge alongside. Use renderDCPowerSim(U, I, R, P) helper.

Step 6 - DC EMF simulator (renderDCEmf): Add SVG showing a battery with internal resistance. Show the EMF source, internal resistance Ri (with heat glow for I^2*Ri loss), external terminal voltage U_kl, and current flow. A voltage bar diagram on the side shows how E splits between Ri voltage drop and terminal voltage. Use renderDCEmfSim(E, Ri, I, Ukl, Ploss, Pload) helper.

Step 7 - Impedans module simulator (after phasor diagram ~line 24330): Add an SVG circuit diagram showing the selected RLC configuration (series or parallel). Components: R as a zigzag/rectangle, L as a coil symbol, C as parallel plates. Animate current flow with speed proportional to |I|. Show voltage drops across each component with proportional bar heights. The phase relationship is already shown in the phasor - this adds the circuit topology. Use renderImpedansSim(state, res) helper.

Step 8 - Trefase module simulator: After results in renderTrefase(), add SVG showing 3-phase star or delta connection. For star: 3 phase wires converging to neutral point with loads on each phase. For delta: triangular loop with loads on each side. Animate current flow in each phase wire with 120-degree offset (using animation-delay). Color each phase (L1=brown, L2=black, L3=grey per IEC). Show voltage labels (UL between phases, UP to neutral). Use renderTrefaseSim(loadType, results) helper.

Step 9 - Voltage Drop simulator (renderVdrop): Add SVG showing a cable run from source to load. The cable is drawn as a long rectangle with a color gradient from green (full voltage) at source end to yellow/red (reduced voltage) at load end. Percentage labels at both ends. Animate current flow through the cable. Cable thickness proportional to cross-section. Use renderVdropSim(Un, vdrop, vdropPercent, length, crossSection) helper.

Step 10 - Short Circuit simulator (renderShortCircuit): Add SVG showing the fault current path: transformer -> cable -> fault point. Show impedances as blocks sized proportionally (Znet, Ztrafo, Zcable). At fault point show a lightning bolt symbol. Animate fault current flow (fast-moving dashes) with thickness proportional to Ik magnitude. Use renderScircuitSim(zNet, zTrafo, zCable, ikMax, ikMin) helper.

Step 11 - Motor Theory simulator (renderMotorteori): Add SVG showing simplified induction motor cross-section: stator (outer ring with coil symbols), rotor (inner ring), air gap. Animate the rotor spinning at speed proportional to (1-slip)*ns. Show stator current flow. Label synchronous speed, actual speed, and slip. Use renderMotorSim(ns, n, slip, torque) helper.

Step 12 - Add tests in test-sld.js: Add test cases verifying that each simulator helper function exists and returns a string containing '<svg'. Test that the SVG contains expected elements (e.g., renderDCOhmSim returns SVG with class sim-wire). Test that renderDCOhm() output includes the simulator SVG. Test edge cases (zero current = no animation, zero resistance = direct short indicator).
acceptance_criteria: Running 'node test-sld.js' passes all tests (existing 1344 + new simulator tests)
Each of the 6 modules (DC with 5 sub-calculators, impedans, trefase, vdrop, scircuit, motorteori) renders an inline SVG circuit simulator below its results
SVG animations use CSS classes that respect prefers-reduced-motion and body.motion-off
All simulator helper functions are pure (take values, return SVG string) and unit-testable
Current flow animation speed varies with the calculated current value
Resistor/load elements show power dissipation via color intensity (blue=cold, red=hot)
Voltage potential shown via color gradient (green=high, red=low) or labeled nodes
No text inputs added - simulators are read-only visual output
Bilingual labels in simulators using tx() function
verification: Run 'node test-sld.js' and confirm all tests pass with 0 failures

Verify new simulator functions exist by grepping: grep -c 'renderDCOhmSim\|renderDCSeriesParallelSim\|renderDCDividerSim\|renderDCPowerSim\|renderDCEmfSim\|renderImpedansSim\|renderTrefaseSim\|renderVdropSim\|renderScircuitSim\|renderMotorSim' el-dimensionering.html
findings: All 11 simulator helper functions implemented and integrated. CSS animations use .sim-wire (marching dashes) and .sim-rotor (spinning) with motion-off support. 1374 tests pass (30 new). The vdrop and scircuit modules use template literals (backtick strings) so simulators are injected via ${} expressions. The trefase simulator only renders for symStar and symDelta load types (asymmetric calculations use local vars in if-blocks). Review fixes applied: (1) isFinite guard in simHeatColor for Ri=0 edge case, (2) unique gradient IDs in renderVdropSim, (3) rotor animation disabled below 10 rpm, (4) .sim-resistor/.sim-source transitions disabled under reduced motion.
